narrative_votes: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
CC: Questioning the measurements and science

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: "rain bombs" and "boiling oceans"
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes the climate movement by using the term 'climate cultists' and implying that their language is 'bizarre' and 'scare' tactics, indicating an attack on the messengers and their methods of raising awareness about climate issues.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text downplays the significance of climate change by suggesting that past predictions have been incorrect, implying that current warnings are also likely to be exaggerated or false.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Gore would know about fossil fuel use, seeing as how he uses lots and lots of it – much more than the average person, in fact – to power his lavish, sprawling estates and fuel his private jets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes Al Gore, an authority figure in the climate movement, for his perceived hypocrisy in using large amounts of fossil fuels despite advocating for their reduction, challenging his integrity and credibility.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: A few years ago, someone (John Nolte?) listed all of their bigger predictions and their track record was 0-47
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text questions the accuracy of climate predictions and science by highlighting a claimed track record of failed predictions, implying that the scientific consensus or methods may be flawed.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: "rain bombs" and "boiling oceans"
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes the climate movement for using exaggerated language to scare people, implying that they are alarmist and dishonest.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Gore would know about fossil fuel use, seeing as how he uses lots and lots of it – much more than the average person, in fact – to power his lavish, sprawling estates and fuel his private jets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes Al Gore, a prominent figure in the climate movement, for his perceived hypocrisy and excessive use of fossil fuels, undermining his credibility and the credibility of the movement.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text downplays the significance of climate change by pointing out that past predictions of catastrophic events have not come true, implying that the current warnings are also exaggerated or unfounded.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: A few years ago, someone (John Nolte?) listed all of their bigger predictions and their track record was 0-47
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text questions the accuracy of climate predictions and the science behind them by highlighting the poor track record of past predictions, implying that the science is flawed or unreliable.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: The legislation contains $369 billion, not including tax credits, in appropriation funds for "renewable energy," meaning windmills, solar panels, and a very dark and cold future for humanity.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text expresses skepticism about the effectiveness and desirability of renewable energy technologies, implying that they are not a viable solution to the energy needs of humanity.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: "rain bombs" and "boiling oceans"
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes the climate movement for using exaggerated language to scare people, implying that they are alarmist and dishonest.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Al Gore, Bill Clinton's former vice president, condemned "greenhouse gas pollution" for "boiling the oceans" and causing "rain bombs," whatever those are supposed to be.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes Al Gore, a prominent figure in the climate movement, for making exaggerated claims, implying that he is incompetent or dishonest.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text downplays the significance of climate change by pointing out that past predictions have not come true, implying that the current warnings are also exaggerated or unfounded.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: A few years ago, someone (John Nolte?) listed all of their bigger predictions and their track record was 0-47
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text questions the accuracy of climate predictions and the science behind them, implying that the measurements and data are unreliable or flawed.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Cheap and abundant oil and natural gas have to go, Gore says – for poor people, anyway – in order for the planet to stop warming.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text expresses skepticism about the feasibility and fairness of transitioning to green technologies, implying that they may not be effective or may harm certain groups, such as the poor.
